title: Field Lines, Repetition, Combination, Structured Fields, and Limits · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first loss-aware bounded field-section model for field lines, repetition, combination, structured fields, and limits. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of loss-aware bounded field-section model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse case-insensitive token field names, colon delimiters, optional field whitespace, and field values while rejecting whitespace before the colon.
Preserve field-line order and repeated values, combining only when that field definition permits it.
Parse Connection options and nominate hop-by-hop fields dynamically instead of relying only on a fixed list.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Field Name Value Grammar
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 6735 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve repeated field lines and reject sections beyond a bounded policy; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Field Name Value Grammar.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse case-insensitive token field names, colon delimiters, optional field whitespace, and field values while rejecting whitespace before the colon. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Field Name Value Grammar, keep Field Name Value Grammar connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
